calendarNotes:
Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 25, 2026 — the latest start date among North Carolina's four largest districts. Wake County starts August 6, Cumberland County starts August 7, and Winston-Salem starts August 10. CMS's later August 25 start reflects the district's calendar structure approved by the Mecklenburg County Board of Education. Spring Break runs March 29–April 2, 2027, and the school year ends June 9, 2027. Note that Good Friday (March 26) is observed as a school holiday the Friday before spring break begins, giving families a de facto four-day weekend before the break week.

Charlotte is one of the fastest-growing large cities in the United States, driven by banking, financial services, and technology. Corporate relocations bring a steady stream of new families into CMS year-round — not just in August. Families transferred by Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Truist, Honeywell, and other major Charlotte employers often arrive mid-year and enroll students outside the August window. Families moving from New York, Ohio, or Chicago frequently expect school to start in late August or after Labor Day — CMS's August 25 start is close to that expectation. However, CMS ends on June 9, later than most relocating families expect — summer camp and childcare should cover through the second week of June.

CMS operates one of the largest magnet school systems in North Carolina, with programs spanning STEM, arts, language immersion, and International Baccalaureate. Magnet school applications typically open in November and close in January for the following school year. Charlotte's growth means popular programs fill quickly — families interested in IB, performing arts, or STEM magnets should attend a fall choice information fair and submit applications before the January deadline.

Charlotte's rapid growth has required CMS to redraw school attendance boundaries multiple times over the past decade as new neighborhoods develop and enrollment shifts. Families buying or renting in Mecklenburg County should verify their assigned school directly at cms.k12.nc.us using their address before finalizing a home purchase or lease — a boundary rezoning since last year may mean a different school assignment than neighbors or online sources indicate.

Families coordinating with relatives or childcare providers in South Carolina suburbs of Charlotte — including Fort Mill, Rock Hill, and Indian Land in York County — should note that South Carolina public schools follow a different academic calendar. SC districts typically start in early August (often before CMS) and may have spring break dates that differ by one to two weeks. The Charlotte metro is genuinely a two-state school zone — never assume calendars align.
